summary: Fully matched the Metal Box item module by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itmetalb.s with src/melee/it/items/itmetalb.c, marking itmetalb.c as Matching in configure.py, and switching obj_files.mk from the assembly object to the C object. The PR also deleted an accidentally leftover asm/melee/it/items/itparasol.s file. Supporting header work added complete itmetalb prototypes and a minimal itcoll.h declaration for it_8027236C.
what_changed: Added a 152-line C implementation in src/melee/it/items/itmetalb.c containing the Metal Box item state table it_803F62C0 and functions it_802953C8 through it_802956EC.
Changed configure.py entry for melee/it/items/itmetalb.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Changed obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/items/itmetalb.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/it/items/itmetalb.s.o.
Deleted asm/melee/it/items/itparasol.s, described in the PR body as an accidentally leftover .s file.
Expanded src/melee/it/items/itmetalb.h from a small public subset to prototypes for all matched local/global functions, while preserving extern ItemStateTable it_803F62C0[].
Added src/melee/it/itcoll.h with include guards and the declaration HSD_GObj* it_8027236C(Item_GObj*).
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt entries at 0x804D5260 and 0x804D5268 from it_804D5260/it_804D5268 object labels to local string labels @219 and @220 with sizes 0x7 and 0x5.
smart_moves: Kept the item state table as structured C: it_803F62C0[] uses ItemStateTable rows with callbacks such as it_8029544C, it_80295454, it_80295458, it_802954C0, it_802954C8, it_802954F8, it_8029557C, it_802955AC, it_802956AC, it_802956B4, and it_802956B8 instead of raw .4byte data.
Modeled assembly bit operations using existing Item bitfields, e.g. xDCE_flag.bits.b7 = 0 and xDCF_flag.bits.b6, making the decompiled code cleaner while still matching.

Reused existing item helper APIs for common state transitions and physics behavior: Item_80268E5C, it_8026B390, it_8026D62C, it_80276CB8, it_80272860, it_8026E15C, it_8026DA08, it_8026E8C4, and it_8026B894.

Used GET_ITEM where the original load pattern benefited from it, while also using gobj->user_data in simpler functions; this suggests matching was tuned per function rather than forced into one style.
Added PAD_STACK(8) in it_802955E0, indicating a deliberate stack-layout matching tactic for a function that calls it_8027236C, ftLib functions, and pl_8003E17C.
Cleaned build metadata and source ownership at the same time as the match, avoiding a stale assembly object and ensuring the matched C file is used by the build.
decomp_lessons: When an item module becomes a full match, update all three ownership points together: remove the module .s, mark the C file Matching in configure.py, and switch obj_files.mk to the C object.
Item state tables can often be represented directly as ItemStateTable arrays; preserving callback order is enough to replace raw data blocks.

For item code, prefer existing item macros and fields such as GET_ITEM, Item_GObj, Item, x40_vel, xCC_item_attr, xDCE_flag, xDCF_flag, and xCEC_fighterGObj when they reproduce the original loads and stores.
Small wrappers returning false or doing only return; should still be typed accurately as bool or void to match the callback table signatures.
Stack padding may be necessary even in readable C when matching item interaction routines that combine local object pointers and multiple library calls.
If a PR uncovers stale assembly from another item, removing it as part of cleanup is acceptable when the PR evidence clearly identifies it as accidentally leftover.
naming_conventions: Unidentified item functions retained address-based names in the it_80295xxx range rather than speculative semantic names.
The matched state table retained the address-based global name it_803F62C0.
Local small-data string symbols in symbols.txt used compiler-style @219 and @220 labels rather than fabricated descriptive names.
The new collision header used the existing lowercase module style and guard form _itcoll_h_.
Function prototypes in itmetalb.h omitted old address comments after expansion, matching a cleaner prototype-list style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced assembly velocity zeroing with chained assignment to it->x40_vel.x/y/z = 0.0f.
Matched item animation/state updates through Item_80268E5C with constants such as ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE and ITEM_DROP_UPDATE instead of raw numeric flags.
Represented fall-speed handling by loading it->xCC_item_attr->x10_fall_speed and x14_fall_speed_max and passing them to it_80272860.
Represented the assembly branch around it_8026DA08 as a straightforward bool return path in it_802955AC.

In it_802955E0, preserved behavior that checks for a non-NULL related GObj, requires classifier HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_FIGHTER and xDCF_flag.bits.b6, calls ftLib_800871A8, then calls pl_8003E17C with ftLib_80086BE0(go) & 0xFF and ftLib_800874BC(go).
Set it->xCEC_fighterGObj = NULL in the non-fighter or flag-false path, matching the original store of zero to offset 0xCEC.
Used PAD_STACK(8) to satisfy stack/register matching constraints in it_802955E0.
Updated symbols.txt small-data entries to string labels and exact string sizes, preventing stale object-symbol assumptions after deleting asm.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or semantically identify itmetalb functions it_802953C8 through it_802956EC.
Check whether the accidental itparasol.s removal interacted with subsequent itparasol matching work.
Search for other item modules using PAD_STACK in callback functions that call ftLib and pl_8003E17C.
Look for follow-up header cleanup around itcoll.h and declarations for it_8027236C.
